mnemonics: S.H.A.R.E - Scheduling Helps All Running Executions (for Multiprocessor)
H.O.M.E - Heterogeneous, On-line, Migration, Even-load (for Multiprocessor Types)
A.S.A.P - Asymmetric, Symmetric, Affinity, Processor (for Scheduling Approaches)
keyPoints: Load sharing distributes work across processors to maintain balance.
Processor affinity (soft or hard) binds a process to a CPU to minimize cache invalidation.
Symmetric Multiprocessing (SMP) allows any processor to run any thread in the system.
Asymmetric Multiprocessing (AMP) designates a specific processor (e.g., the master) for scheduling decisions.
Scheduling in multiprocessor systems is more complex due to issues like cache coherency and processor affinity.
commonMistakes: Assuming scheduling algorithms for single processors directly apply to multiple processors without modification.
Overlooking the performance impact of cache misses when a process migrates between processors (ignoring processor affinity).
Confusing Symmetric Multiprocessing (SMP) with Asymmetric Multiprocessing (AMP) architectures.
